title: On A Midsummer Eve
content:
I idly cut a parsley stalk,
    And blew therein towards the moon;
    I had not thought what ghosts would walk
    With shivering footsteps to my tune.

    I went, and knelt, and scooped my hand
    As if to drink, into the brook,
    And a faint figure seemed to stand
    Above me, with the bygone look.

    I lipped rough rhymes of chance, not choice,
    I thought not what my words might be;
    There came into my ear a voice
    That turned a tenderer verse for me.
meaning:
"On A Midsummer Eve" is a quintessential example of Thomas Hardy's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
